# Source Card: Application of GFR estimating equations to children with normal, near-normal, or discordant GFR

## Summary
This single-center prospective study (n=29 children with mild CKD, stages 1-2) compared six pediatric eGFR equations against measured plasma iohexol clearance (iGFR) in the normal/near-normal GFR range. It establishes a >=15 mL/min/1.73 m2 gap between creatinine- and cystatin-C-based eGFR as clinically meaningful discordance, recommends the CKiD U25-combined formula for screening children for low GFR (eGFR <90 mL/min/1.73 m2), and documents that all equations disagreed with measured GFR in over a third of participants. For a CDS module this is evidence on equation selection and discordance handling, not an executable rule.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) 22 of 29 children had creatinine vs cystatin-C eGFR discordant by >=15 mL/min/1.73 m2 — locator: Abstract Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:d5e42cd7e0b5316fde70e0ae0684c4fb8a36da8bb4badd1b4e111df748242b48]"
- (ev_002) U25 most accurately identified children with eGFR <90 mL/min/1.73 m2 — locator: Abstract Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:8dc1898e74d4ab0597d3f7f2663fa4e1677204021e667510e528407911806854]"
- (ev_003) Discordance direction selects the best-matching equation vs 4-point iohexol GFR — locator: Abstract Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:9adf52efd7d96e554b749377458750e594db9cacfe26767c43651e627b456a9a]"
- (ev_004) Recommend CKiD U25-combined to screen for low GFR; U25-combined or FAS-combined for longitudinal change — locator: Abstract Conclusions — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:cc306f3c5f7413ebcab5cfea878c0c41a51e411c0959d381122fa8f84633a9f8]"
- (ev_005) All formulas discordant from measured GFR in over a third of participants — locator: Abstract Conclusions — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:bdc4c60db205745b06d4d489789fa45ba79752787bd6a9b9ba09c536b9a22dd9]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: children with mild CKD (stages 1-2), normal/near-normal GFR; n=29; sex and specific age band not reported in the abstract (U25 equations apply to under-25 CKiD cohorts).
- Assay/method dependence is central: results are the difference between creatinine-based and cystatin-C-based eGFR, benchmarked against measured plasma iohexol clearance (2-point and 4-point). Equation choice (CKiD U25 x3, FAS-combined, EKFC-creatinine, CKD-EPI cystatin-C) materially changes the estimate. Any CDS use must fix both the assay basis and the equation.
- Lifecycle: published 2023-07 (Pediatr Nephrol 38(12):4051-4059); no retirement/supersession recorded.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- The eGFR <90 mL/min/1.73 m2 screen flag and the >=15 mL/min/1.73 m2 discordance threshold function as candidate "low-GFR / investigate discordance" triggers, but the source frames them as screening/equation-selection guidance, not a referral directive. Any referral trigger is an implementation decision for the CDS converter, not asserted by this source.

## Limitations & conflicts
- Full text is paywalled (Springer); evidence points are drawn from the published abstract only. Body-of-paper numbers (exact ages, per-equation bias/precision values, iohexol protocol thresholds) are NOT locatable here and are recorded as GAPs: specific age band not locatable in abstract; per-equation numeric bias values not locatable in abstract.
- Small single-center sample (n=29) at the normal/near-normal GFR range limits generalizability; the authors themselves note all formulas fail in >1/3 of cases, so no single equation should be treated as ground truth.
- No direct conflict recorded with other sources; note that this paper's U25-combined screening recommendation is a refinement over the older bedside Schwartz equation and CKD-EPI cystatin-C, which it evaluates rather than endorses. If a sibling source card asserts a different preferred pediatric eGFR equation, preserve both as a conflict — do not average.
